explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **text segments describing the release, publication, or announcement of official information** (such as reports, data, laws, findings, or statements from authorities, governments, or organizations).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across multiple languages (English, Spanish, Polish, Indonesian, Italian, Portuguese) and domains including government policy, scientific research, legal proceedings, economic data, military activity, and news reporting. It activates on phrases like "released by," "published in," "dado a conocer," "ogłosiła," "publicó," and "desveló," often near references to specific entities (e.g., China's National Mining Safety Administration, Justice Department, 'Nature Communications').

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The high-activation texts consistently feature an *act of disclosure* by an official or institutional source. The low-activation examples lack this; they describe ongoing events, opinions, general narratives, or personal statements without framing them as a formal release of information (e.g., "a burglar was caught," "he endorsed," "a video surfaced").

4. **Closing interpretation**: This neuron detects linguistic markers of formal information becoming public. It focuses on the semantic frame of **institutional disclosure**, serving as a feature for tracking source attribution and the dissemination of authoritative content in news and documentary text.

results.actual_text: informó a la Comisión Nacional del Mercado de Valores que el fallo, emitido pese a que los peritos judiciales se pronunci
results.plain_text: La administración de la compañía continúa en poder de los actuales gestores. Imagen de obra en El Zapotillo. EL INFORMADOR / ARCHIVO La determinación se concretó tras el impago de los intereses a bonos emitidos por la compañía CIUDAD DE MÉXICO (21/DIC/2016).- A petición de Banco Base, uno de sus acreedores, el Juzgado Sexto del Distrito en Materia Civil de la Ciudad de México declaró en concurso de acreedores a Abengoa México.  La determinación del juez se concretó luego del impago de los intereses correspondientes a bonos emitidos por la compañía.  En Jalisco, Abengoa México es la encargada de la construcción del acueducto El Zapotillo, cuya función será dotar de agua a la Zona Metropolitana de Guadalajara (ZMG).  La empresa, cuya hermana tiene su corporativo en España, informó a la Comisión Nacional del Mercado de Valores que el fallo, emitido pese a que los peritos judiciales se pronunciaron en contra de dicha declaración, no supone la pérdida de la administración por parte de los actuales gestores de dicha compañía.  Además, calificó la decisión judicial de “inusual e inesperada”, trabaja junto con sus asesores para “adoptar las medidas necesarias para proteger sus intereses”.  “Esta decisión se refiere únicamente a Abengoa México, no afectando a ninguna de sus filiales ni a ninguna otra compañía del grupo Abengoa en México ni en ningún otro país”, subrayó la compañía, que agregó que ya analiza las implicaciones del fallo y las posibles “alternativas de situación”.  Los peritos o visitadores judiciales habían emitido en dos ocasiones anteriores un informe contrario a la petición inicial de Banco Base, en el que se concluye que Abengoa México no se encontraba en una situación concursal.  Abengoa México aseguró que ya había llegado a un acuerdo de reestructuración con una amplia mayoría de los tenedores de dichos bonos, según la misma fuente.  En septiembre de 2011, la Comisión Nacional del Agua (Conagua) informó que Abengoa México construiría el acueducto El Zapotillo, desde la presa del mismo nombre a León, Guanajuato, los altos de Jalisco y la ZMG.  El proyecto de la presa El Zapotillo tiene el objetivo de dotar de agua potable a la ZMG “mediante la regulación de volúmenes que serán enviados por el cauce del Río Verde hasta la presa derivadora El Purgatorio, cuya licitación está en proceso de elaboración”, informó entonces la Conagua.  Apenas en noviembre pasado, la multinacional española Abengoa aprobó un plan de reestructuración con el que evitó el concurso de acreedores justo un año después de que se encontrara al borde de la quiebra.  Con información de EFE  EN DETALLE  Gana proyecto por sinergia  Abengoa México fue fundada en el año de 1981. Obtuvo los primeros contratos con la iniciativa privada para más tarde posicionar su liderazgo creando proyectos a lo largo del país para facilitar el desarrollo sostenible en los sectores energéticos.  Se posiciona dentro de las empresas privadas líderes del mercado eléctrico y de generación de energía con la construcción del campo híbrido solar-gas Agua Prieta II (14 MW) y de la Central de Ciclo Combinado de Centro Morelos (640 MW).  De acuerdo con su página corporativa, la sinergia de Abengoa México con sus otras filiales en el resto mundo da lugar al otorgamiento de la concesión del Acueducto de Zapotillo, el mayor proyecto hidráulico en la última década.  CIUDAD DE MÉXICO (20/DIC/2016).- Alejandro Maza es fundador de OPI, una ''startup'' dedicada al análisis de grandes cantidades de información que sirve a instituciones privadas y públicas para la toma de decisiones. SUN EL INFORMADOR valora la expresión libre de los usuarios en el sitio web y redes sociales del medio, pero aclara que la responsabilidad de los comentarios se atribuye a cada autor, al tiempo que exhorta a una comunicación respetuosa. En caso de considerar que algún comentario no debería mostrarse por ofender a otras personas, instituciones, o ir contra la Ley, cualquier lector puede denunciarlo utilizando el botón de a la derecha de cada comentario. PARA MOSTRAR LOS COMENTARIOS DE ESTA NOTA PULSA EN ESTE AVISO

results.actual_text: Bloque Stabroek”, precisó Exxon Mobil en un comunicado difundido en su página web. La empresa no dijo si los dos buques,
results.plain_text: Redacción Versión Final @VersionFinal Archivo Correo Imprimir La petrolera estadounidense Exxon Mobil aseguró que las actividades de perforación en aguas de Guyana no han sido afectadas tras un incidente el fin de semana entre la Armada de Venezuela y dos barcos de una de sus contratistas que adelantaban estudios sísmicos en otra zona. Las labores de Exxon se mantenían en los 10 hallazgos petroleros que opera frente a las costas de Guyana, incluso en la zona más cercana al bloque Stabroek, donde el sábado fueron detenidos los estudios que adelantaban las embarcaciones, informó la compañía. Según reseñó Reuters, Guyana ha dicho que el incidente con la marina del país vecino ocurrió dentro de sus aguas territoriales, aunque el Gobierno venezolano indicó que fue en su espacio marítimo. “Las perforaciones de exploración y desarrollo continúan en la zona sureste del Bloque Stabroek”, precisó Exxon Mobil en un comunicado difundido en su página web. La empresa no dijo si los dos buques, que adelantaban los estudios exploratorios y son propiedad de la petrolera noruega Petroleum Geo-Services, tenían planes de reanudar sus labores en el área donde fueron interceptados. El Gobierno de Venezuela envió el miércoles una nota de protesta a Guyana por la decisión del país de permitir que “transnacionales” realicen, sin consulta, exploraciones “unilaterales y arbitrarias” y otras actividades en el área, dijo un comunicado del Ministerio de Relaciones Exteriores. Además resaltó que previamente protestó ante Georgetown por un “aviso a los marineros” emitido el 6 de diciembre en el que se informaba al público que la filial de Exxon en Guyana haría los estudios sísmicos hasta el 31 de diciembre. El aviso publicado en el sitio web del Departamento de Administración Marítima de Guyana tenía fecha del 7 de diciembre y decía que el procedimiento estaba programado para un período de seis meses. Ambas naciones mantienen una disputa territorial por casi un siglo y los descubrimientos de petróleo que hizo Exxon avivaron las tensiones entre los gobiernos. 2017 Copyright Versión Final C.A.Todos los derechos reservados

results.actual_text: of the Global Health Summit, the G20 leaders on May 21 issued a joint declaration aimed at coordinating worldwide efforts against the COVID-19 pandemic.
results.plain_text: IMAGE: UNSPLASH/TWITTER  At the close of the Global Health Summit, the G20 leaders on May 21 issued a joint declaration aimed at coordinating worldwide efforts against the COVID-19 pandemic. According to a press release, the G20 leaders noted that the pandemic continues to be a global health and socio-economic crisis, with disproportionate direct and indirect effects on the most vulnerable as well as healthcare workers. They added that the pandemic will not be over until all countries are able to bring the disease under control and therefore, “large-scale, global, safe, effective and equitable vaccination in combination with appropriate other public health measures remains our top priority”.  “We affirm our support for efforts to strengthen supply chains and boost and diversify global vaccine-manufacturing capacity, including for the materials needed to produce vaccines, including by sharing risks, and welcome the vaccines technology transfer hub launched by the WHO," the Rome Declaration read.   This very first G20 summit on health marks the beginning of a new chapter in global health policy. — Ursula von der Leyen (@vonderleyen) May 21, 2021  World leaders strongly committed to multilateralism and global cooperation in health. As per the press note, the declaration is made up of 16 “mutually reinforcing principles" that reconfirm their commitment to “global solidarity, equity, and multilateral cooperation" and to promoting "sustained financing for global health”. The G20 leaders will support and enhance the existing multilateral health architecture. They will promote the multilateral trading system and the importance of open, resilient, diversified, secure, efficient and reliable global supply chains across the whole value chain related to health emergencies. The leaders will enable equitable, affordable, timely, global access to high-quality, safe and effective prevention, detection and response tools. They said that the group will support low- and middle-income countries to build expertise and develop local and regional manufacturing capacities for tools. They will even facilitate data sharing, capacity building, licensing agreements, and voluntary technology and know-how transfers on mutually agreed terms. Further, they will be investing in the worldwide health and care workforce and also in adequate resourcing, training and staffing of diagnostic public health laboratories. They will increase the effectiveness of preparedness and response measures by supporting and promoting meaningful and inclusive dialogue with local communities, civil society, frontline workers, vulnerable groups. And they will seek to ensure the effectiveness of financing mechanisms.

predictions.reasoning: The G20 leaders issued a joint declaration, a high-level official statement from an international forum.

results.actual_text: Tony Bellew seeks to put 'final nail in the coffin' of David Haye
results.plain_text: Tony Bellew seeks to put 'final nail in the coffin' of David Haye's career after threats before first fight had traumatic effect on his family  David Haye threatened to put Tony Bellew in hospital before first encounter Bellew ended up stopping opponent and faces Haye in rematch on December 17 'Bomber' is still incensed with Haye for traumatic effect words had on his family He said: 'That gob***** made out like it was going to be glorious to hurt me' Tony Bellew is determined to end David Haye's career because of the trauma his intense threats inflicted on his young family. The 34-year-old Bellew instead insists he is nearing the 'finishing line' but a second defeat of Haye would also likely end the latter's ambitions. Tony Bellew is still furious with David Haye's threats before their first fight in March The 'Bomber' stopped Haye and is aiming to finish his opponent's career in the rematch During his reign as the WBA heavyweight champion, the 37-year-old Haye's career was so widely celebrated that he became one of Britain's best-known sportsmen, and it is that profile that contributed to Bellew's concerns. He had long fought to keep his profession a secret from his three young children, but even after his successes meant he could no longer do so, it was only in the build-up to their fight in March that Haye's menacing remarks disturbed them. 'To put my kids what he put them through: he's just a k*** of the highest order,' Bellew said. 'He should never have said the things he said: saying my kids are going to visit me in hospital. 'It'll never go away now that he's said it. That gob***** made out like it was going to be glorious to hurt me. 'I sent (my family) to Dubai before that fight and when they came back, the first thing my son said to his mother was 'I can't wait to go to school and tell everyone; I told them all he'd win'. Bellew and Haye put on a thoroughly entertaining fight charged with animosity 'So obviously kids are telling him I'm going to get beat up, or whatever. It's not nice at all, but there's not long left; I can see the finishing line. 'It's not nice when your kids are having to answer to people at school, it breaks my heart to know he told people 'I told you my dad's going to win', so kids had questioned him. 'That's just kids, they don't see any harm in that, but they're going to see harm when they've seen some gob***** saying he's going to put me in a coma. That's what this man's capable of. 'My missus (also) knows it's being said: she was in tears the night she went to Dubai. When you're fighting at heavyweight for the first time your missus and your children worry. 'Dad, you're not that big; he's massive'. It gets personal, and it gets even worse when a gob***** says what he says.' Haye tore his achilles but fought on, eventually coming unstuck against his foe Bellew spoke of the effects Haye's pledge to hospitalise him had on his young family Bellew reassures his children - 12-year-old Corey, nine-year-old Cobey and four-year-old Carter - by promising he will soon retire. 'My son had known I was a boxer for quite some time but he'd never heard a fighter say he was going to leave his father in hospital, they were going to hurt his dad,' he said. 'That was the first time he'd worried. 'They all know now but they've been told 'Dad's almost at the finish line, so don't stress'. 'I want to win more than anything, but I'll take great pleasure in knowing I was the man who put the final nail in the coffin of David Haye's career.' results.actual_text: Sweden’s chief epidemiologist Anders Tegnell has warned against the strategy of attaining
results.plain_text: Sweden’s chief epidemiologist Anders Tegnell has warned against the strategy of attaining herd immunity in apparent change of heart. In March, Dr Tegnell appeared to ask whether a higher death rate among older people might be acceptable if it led to faster herd immunity. However in comments to German newspaper Die Zeit, Dr Tegnell stated “striving for herd immunity is neither ethical nor otherwise justifiable”. Sweden’s daily coronavirus infections have climbed to a new high while several regions advised residents to avoid public transport and venues to slow the transmission. The Swedish Public Health Agency said that 3254 cases were confirmed in the past 24 hours, a new record since the onset of the pandemic. It was the third record number in a matter of days and came as the country of 10.3 million has registered roughly 121,100 infections and 5934 coronavirus-related deaths in total. Sweden has until recently had “a fairly slow increase (of infections) compared to most other countries,” Dr Tegnell said at a press conference. “We’re beginning to approach the ceiling for what the healthcare system can handle. “Together, as during the spring, we can push down this curve and avoid the strain on healthcare.” Dr Tegnell said the Swedish system of “voluntary measures” had worked well and many people heeded the recommendations. The number of COVID-19 patients in intensive care units (ICU) was still relatively low – about 15 per cent of the current ICU capacity, said Thomas Linden with the National Board of Health and Welfare. Sweden has shunned lockdowns throughout the pandemic. However, several regions – including the most populous areas comprising the main cities of Stockholm and Gothenburg in western Sweden – advised residents to refrain from visits to shopping centres, museums, libraries, swimming pools and fitness centres until November 18. Other advice was for people to avoid meeting with others outside their own household and to work from home where possible, Dr Tegnell said. During the pandemic, Sweden has kept schools and kindergartens open but limited public gatherings to 50 participants, visits in care homes were until recently banned and self service in restaurants and bars suspended. -with agencies
